title: Satiating Algerian Lentils Stir-fry
description: A savory, slightly bitter lentil stir-fry topped with a rich but low-sugar custard, perfect for a post-workout meal.

ingredients.name: creme patissiere
ingredients.amount: 200ml milk, 2 egg yolks, 1 tbsp flour, 1 tsp vanilla, 1 tsp stevia

instructions: Rinse 200g red lentils under cold water until clear, then set aside.
In a medium saucepan, bring 600ml water to a boil, add the lentils, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 18 minutes until tender and slightly broken.
While lentils cook, heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add diced onion and minced garlic to the skillet and sauté for 2 minutes until translucent and fragrant.
Toss in chopped carrot and diced tomato, cooking for 4 minutes until the vegetables soften.
Stir in cumin, coriander, paprika, and salt and pepper, cooking for an additional minute to bloom the spices.
Drain the cooked lentils and add them to the skillet, mixing well with the vegetables and spices; cook for 3 more minutes to combine flavors.

Meanwhile, prepare the creme patissiere by heating 200ml milk in a small saucepan until steaming; whisk in 2 egg yolks, 1 tbsp flour, 1 tsp vanilla, and 1 tsp stevia, then cook over low heat until thickened, stirring constantly.
Pour the warm custard over the lentil stir-fry, gently stir to coat, and serve immediately.
